title: Colon Collection and Preparation for Index Calculation and Immunohistochemistry
author: Amandine EVEN
materials: The colon is weighed with a precision scale.

steps.title: The mouse is weighed, which is...
steps.instruction: The mouse is weighed, which is necessary for subsequent calculation of the colon index.

steps.title: If present, feces are carefully removed
steps.instruction: If present, feces are carefully removed. To do this, gently slide a forceps along the colon from the proximal to the distal part to expel the feces.

steps.title: After an appropriate euthanasia, the caecum...
steps.instruction: After an appropriate euthanasia, the caecum and the colon are dissected from the mouse. The colon corresponds to the section between the cecum and the anus.

steps.title: The index is calculated as the...
steps.instruction: The index is calculated as the colon mass (mg)/mouse body mass (g).

steps.title: Colons are sectioned in two with...
steps.instruction: Colons are sectioned in two with a scalpel blade - proximal portion, from the part which followed the caecum to the middle - distal portion from the middle to the part which was connected to the anus

steps.title: Colon sections are maintained in a...
steps.instruction: Colon sections are maintained in a petri dish full of cold 1X PBS.

steps.title: For futur analyses of colon RNA...
steps.instruction: For futur analyses of colon RNA or protein , sections are directly transfered into dry 2 mL tubes and transfered on dry ice. After the collection of all samples, the tubes are transfered to -80 °C .

steps.title: With a 10 mL syringe and...
steps.instruction: With a 10 mL syringe and a 21G needle, flush the inside of the colon with cold 1X PBS until it is completely clean. Be careful not to damage the tissues. Apply a steady flow of cold PBS to distend the colon and avoid touching the tissue with the needle.

steps.title: For subsequent colon immunohistopathology or immunohistochemistry...
steps.instruction: For subsequent colon immunohistopathology or immunohistochemistry , tissues need to be fixed.

steps.title: For immunohistopathology, sections are transfered into...
steps.instruction: For immunohistopathology, sections are transfered into 2 mL tubes containing cold 4% PFA and are kept at 4 °C 1-2 days before embedding.

steps.title: For immunohistochemistry, sections are directly inserted...
steps.instruction: For immunohistochemistry, sections are directly inserted into a mold and transfered into a bottle of cold formalin. Bottle are kept at 4 °C 1-2 days before embedding.

steps.title: The lenght of the colon is...
steps.instruction: The lenght of the colon is mesured with a ruler from the caecum to the end. A photography is done with the ruler for record.

steps.title: Excess 1X PBS is dried by...
steps.instruction: Excess 1X PBS is dried by very quickly applying the section to a Kimwipe and the tissue is stored.
